stem: A decision-maker must choose between two options. Option A (planting wheat) yields a guaranteed return of $137K. Option B (planting corn) depends on rainfall: with probability 43-63% (uncertain), it yields $369K; otherwise it yields $52K. Compute the expected value of each option and determine which is better. How does the uncertainty in rainfall affect the recommendation?

ground_truth.answer: EV(A) = $137K (fixed). EV(B) ranges from $188.3K to $251.7K. Point estimate: $220.0K. Option B is preferred at most assumptions (EV_B > EV_A when P(good) > 26.8%).

solution_steps: The decision switches at P(good) = 26.8%, which falls outside the uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Picking a single probability and giving a definitive recommendation without sensitivity analysis
Not computing the breakeven probability where the optimal decision switches
Ignoring the range of outcomes and focusing only on expected values (risk-neutral assumption)
